description: 15 critical handoffs between departments at Lakeshore Development. Each handoff represents a point where information, approval, or deliverable passes from one department to another. Failures at these points cause cascading delays and financial impact.

handoffs.name: Checklist Done Payment Trigger
handoffs.trigger: Supervisor/Monitor marks phase checklist done in Builder Trend
handoffs.impact: Sub payment released immediately. Sub will NOT return after payment, so verification must be thorough before marking done. Incorrect marking = paying for incomplete work with no recourse.
handoffs.sla: 24 hours from service completion to checklist verification
handoffs.failure_mode: If checklist done without proper verification, sub gets paid for incomplete work. New monitors without adequate training are highest risk. This is the single most financially dangerous handoff.
handoffs.frequency: Daily (multiple per day across all active houses)
handoffs.automation_opportunity: Photo evidence required before marking done; supervisor sign-off on monitor decisions

handoffs.name: Draw Release
handoffs.trigger: Financial milestone reached; draw payment received from client/investor
handoffs.impact: Draw number unlocks specific phase trades. 1st draw: up to Robintec. 2nd draw: rest of Phase 1. 4th draw: Phase 2 (Drawing, 3Rs, doors/windows). Without draw release, entire construction phase is blocked regardless of operational readiness.
handoffs.sla: Notification to Scheduling within 24 hours of draw receipt
handoffs.failure_mode: If draw delayed or Scheduling not notified, house sits idle with sub crews potentially reassigned to other jobs. No visibility for Scheduling on when draw will be released.
handoffs.frequency: Per house, 4-6 times during construction cycle
handoffs.automation_opportunity: Real-time draw status dashboard visible to Scheduling; automatic notification on draw receipt

handoffs.name: Survey Received
handoffs.trigger: Survey document received from surveyor and tagged in Builder Trend
handoffs.impact: House becomes visible in scheduler's workflow. Without 'Survey Received' tag in BT, Scheduling team does not know the house exists. House can sit in limbo indefinitely.
handoffs.sla: Tag must be placed same day survey is received
handoffs.failure_mode: If tag not placed, house is invisible to Scheduling. Start of construction delayed by days or weeks with no alert to anyone. Silent failure.
handoffs.automation_opportunity: Auto-create house record when first draw received; eliminate dependency on manual tag

handoffs.name: Floor Plan Complete
handoffs.trigger: Architectural floor plan finalized and approved by Camila Martins
handoffs.impact: Root document for entire construction pipeline. Delay or version error contaminates ALL downstream departments: budget calculated wrong, permit applied with wrong plan, takeoff quantities incorrect, materials purchased incorrectly.
handoffs.sla: Spec homes: 1-2 weeks. Custom homes: 2-4 weeks (client approval cycles)
handoffs.failure_mode: Wrong version reaching field causes construction errors. Changes during construction not communicated to PM/Supervisors. No formal version control system in place.
handoffs.automation_opportunity: Digital version control with seal; only active version distributed; automatic notification of revisions to all stakeholders

handoffs.name: Takeoff Complete
handoffs.trigger: Material takeoff completed from engineering documents using BlueBeam
handoffs.impact: Quantities drive all purchasing decisions. Incorrect takeoff = wrong materials purchased, budget miscalculated, field shortages or surplus. Changes to takeoff do NOT automatically notify Budget team.
handoffs.sla: Within 1 week of receiving engineering documents
handoffs.failure_mode: Takeoff updates not notified to Budget. Budget calculated with outdated quantities. For custom homes, client changes require complete re-takeoff each time.
handoffs.frequency: Per house; custom homes may have multiple revisions
handoffs.automation_opportunity: Automatic notification to Budget and Compras when takeoff is versioned; delta report showing what changed

handoffs.name: Permit Approved
handoffs.trigger: County issues permit with stamped documents
handoffs.impact: Stamped documents must reach field supervisors for legal construction. Without permit, construction cannot begin. Permit expires in 180 days if construction not started.
handoffs.sla: Stamped docs delivered to field within 48 hours of permit issuance
handoffs.failure_mode: Documents with wrong version reaching field. Permit expiration not tracked (Marion County does not send email warnings). Conditions/holds not communicated.
handoffs.automation_opportunity: Automatic expiration tracking with 30/60/90 day alerts; digital distribution of stamped docs

handoffs.name: Contract Signed
handoffs.trigger: Client/investor signs construction contract via Brokermint
handoffs.impact: Contract signature triggers draw collection from Accounts Receivable (Cinthia). Delay in notification = delay in cash flow = delay in construction start.
handoffs.sla: Same-day notification from Permits to Financeiro
handoffs.failure_mode: If Permits does not notify Financeiro of signed contract, draw collection does not begin. BT notifications unreliable for this purpose.
handoffs.frequency: Per house, once at contract signing
handoffs.automation_opportunity: Automatic trigger from digital signature platform to Financeiro system

handoffs.name: Inspection Result
handoffs.trigger: County inspector completes inspection and posts result on county portal
handoffs.impact: Pass = proceed to next phase. Fail = rework required (Supervisao coordinates with sub). Hold = entire house frozen until resolved. No automatic notification when hold is removed.
handoffs.sla: Result communicated to Scheduling same day (typically next business day after inspection)
handoffs.failure_mode: Hold on house not communicated centrally. Scheduling continues booking services on a held house. No alert when hold is lifted, requiring daily manual checks on county portal.
handoffs.frequency: Multiple per house (8-12 inspections per construction cycle)
handoffs.automation_opportunity: Automated county portal monitoring with push notifications on result changes and hold removals

handoffs.name: Tug Side Info
handoffs.trigger: Dandara determines which side of the house the electrical tug should be installed
handoffs.impact: Critical for RF Electric scheduling. Installation on wrong side = significant additional cost and rescheduling. Sub must know before showing up.
handoffs.sla: Before RF Electric is scheduled (minimum 3 business days lead time)
handoffs.failure_mode: Wrong tug side communicated via informal Slack/email. Sub installs on wrong side, requiring costly reinstallation. No formal confirmation mechanism.
handoffs.automation_opportunity: Mandatory confirmed tug side field before RF Electric can be scheduled in system

handoffs.name: Water/Sewer System Info
handoffs.trigger: Dandara receives UAL (Utilities Availability Letter) defining water/sewer system
handoffs.impact: Determines which documentation is needed for the building permit application. Wrong system type = incorrect permit application, potential rejection or rework.
handoffs.failure_mode: Dandara discovers system type but communicates manually to Permits via WhatsApp. If message missed, permit application may have wrong documentation.
handoffs.automation_opportunity: Automatic notification from Utilities to Permits when UAL received; system type as mandatory field

handoffs.name: Client Approval for Cost Override
handoffs.trigger: Client approves exceeding cost (water/sewer extension, special requirements) via email or contract amendment
handoffs.impact: Without client approval, work cannot proceed on cost-exceeding items. Utilities, Permits, or Construction may be blocked waiting for client decision.
handoffs.sla: Variable (depends on client response time; CR follows up weekly)
handoffs.failure_mode: Client hold communicated via informal channels. Multiple departments may not know a hold exists. Work proceeds without approval, creating financial dispute.
handoffs.frequency: Per house when cost overruns occur (common in custom homes)
handoffs.automation_opportunity: Digital approval workflow with client portal; automatic hold placed on house until approval received

handoffs.name: Load Calculation Complete
handoffs.trigger: Supervisor completes load calculation for pad elevation using equipment on-site
handoffs.impact: Phase 1 gate. Without load calculation, pad cannot be scheduled. Pad delays cascade to compact test, form, and entire Phase 1. Equipment shortage is the primary bottleneck.
handoffs.sla: Within 48 hours of clear lot completion
handoffs.failure_mode: Lack of specialized equipment delays load calculation. Supervisor busy with other houses. No visibility for Scheduling on when calculation will be completed.
handoffs.automation_opportunity: Equipment availability calendar; automatic alert when clear lot done to trigger load calculation scheduling

handoffs.name: CO Issued
handoffs.trigger: County issues Certificate of Occupancy after all final inspections pass
handoffs.impact: House is legally ready for delivery and occupancy. Without CO, house cannot be sold or occupied. Impact fees from Financeiro can block Final Electric inspection which blocks CO.
handoffs.sla: CO communicated to Warranty/Closing same day
handoffs.failure_mode: Impact fees not paid by Financeiro blocks Final Electric, which blocks CO. No cross-department visibility on this dependency. Inspection scheduled in vain.
handoffs.frequency: Per house, once at end of construction
handoffs.automation_opportunity: Automatic CO notification to Warranty, Client Relations, and Financeiro; pre-check on impact fee status before scheduling final inspection

handoffs.name: Land Ready for Construction
handoffs.trigger: Land development complete: underground infrastructure approved by county
handoffs.impact: Formal transition from land development to vertical construction. Underground infrastructure (sewer, water, streets) must be approved before any house construction can begin on the lot.
handoffs.sla: Formal handoff meeting/notification when infrastructure passes final inspection
handoffs.failure_mode: Informal handoff. Building starts before all underground infrastructure is verified. Permits applied before land development approvals are finalized.
handoffs.frequency: Per subdivision (affects multiple lots at once)
handoffs.automation_opportunity: Lot status tracking with automatic unlock for building permits when land development milestones completed

handoffs.name: Budget Approved by Client
handoffs.trigger: Budget for custom home finalized and ready for client presentation
handoffs.data: house_id, budget_document, cost_breakdown, allowances, exclusions, total_estimate
handoffs.impact: Without budget approval, contract cannot be signed. Entire custom home cycle depends on this approval. Budget is an estimate under cost-plus model (CEO Bernardo's philosophy), not a cap.
handoffs.sla: Budget presented to client within 1 week of completion; client response variable
handoffs.failure_mode: Budget calculated 'in the dark' at early stages. Client changes requirements after budget approval, requiring re-budget. No formal tracking of pre-contract projects.
handoffs.automation_opportunity: Pre-contract CRM module with budget versioning; client portal for budget review and digital approval
